page.text: M/s. BHARAT PETROLEUM CORPN. LTD
MUMBAI REFINERY
MAHUL,
MUMBAI - 400074
India
Subject :- Open Tender for " #LSTK Contract for CDU-3 F-101 Crude Heater
Revamp Job" at BPCL Mumbai Refinery.
You are invited to quote online through E-Procurement website gem.gov.in
"On-Two-Bid Basis" for the subject Services as per the enclosed Tender
Documents.
Your quoted rates shall be inclusive of all applicable taxes and duties
including GST (CGST+SGST / UTGST) / IGST. Please note that submission of this
form along with Technical / Un-priced Bid is mandatory. Your rates shall
remain firm till completion of work. The applicability of GST (CGST+SGST /
UTGST) / IGST as a percentage rate of total quoted price shall be clearly
mentioned in this form. If the information is not provided, it will be assumed
that GST is not applicable. No claim for GST (CGST+SGST / UTGST) / IGST will
be entertained subsequently. Please note that registration for GST (CGST+SGST
/ UTGST) / IGST is mandatory for receipt of GST payments from BPCL. In absence
of valid registration, your offer will remain invalid and will not be
considered for evaluation. In case GST (CGST+SGST / UTGST) / IGST is not
applicable, please indicate the same as "Nil" while submitting this form.
GST (CGST+SGST / UTGST) / IGST, if applicable shall be paid after verifying
GST (CGST+SGST / UTGST) / IGST Registration no. Invoicing shall be done for
the subject job as per approved statutory format for invoice (i.e. invoice
should clearly specify billing address of Bidder / Address of service receiver
/ GST registration nos. of Bidder and service receiver / applicable SAC no. /
GST rate applicable / invoice no. / invoice date / etc.
Any statutory variation in GST (CGST+SGST / UTGST) / IGST or introduction of
any new statutory taxes and duties within the contractual completion period
shall be to BPCL account, against submission of documentary evidence for
substantiating the variation by way of relevant notification. However in case
of delay in completion period beyond the contractual date, for reasons
attributable to Bidder, any increase in these rates or any new statutory taxes
and duties introduced during the period beyond the contractual completion date
shall be borne by the Bidder, whereas any decrease passed on to the owner.
To,
M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE
MUMBAI . India - 400038.
Ph:,Fax:.
Quotation Due Date/Time:29.04.2026/14:00:00
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
1000454616 / N19-E&C/PROJ TECH SERV
RANJEET KUMAR/022-41073281
Fax : -
Email:ranjeetkumar@bharatpetroleum.in
Process for release of GST amount shall be as per clause 86 of GCC.
Building and other Construction Workers Cess shall be applicable as per clause
no 100 of GCC. Bidders are therefore requested to take into consideration
above Cess (After assessment if applicable) while quoting.
BID VALIDITY: Tender submitted by tenderers shall remain valid for acceptance
for a period of four months from the date of opening of the tender (Technical
Bid in the case of two bid). The tenderer shall not be entitled during the
said period of four months, without the consent in writing of the Owner, to
revoke, or cancel his tender or vary the tender given or any term thereof. In
case of tenderer revoking or canceling his tender, varying any terms in regard
thereof without the consent of Owner in writing, appropriate penal action will
be taken by BPCL as deemed fit including putting the tenderer/contractor on
'Holiday listing'/'Delisting' barring the tenderer/contractor from
participating in future tenders for an appropriate period from the date of
revocation/cancellation/varying the terms. Further in the case of contractors
who are not registered with BPCL, the earnest money deposited by him will be
forfeited. Once the quotation is accepted the rates quoted shall be firm till
the entire work is completed.
Hard copies of the bids shall not be submitted, unless specially asked for by
BPCL.
Pre bid Meeting for discussion on tender terms and conditions shall be on date
and venue as mentioned in the etender. Bidders to ensure submission of their
tender queries for clarification during Pre Bid Meeting before 10:00 hrs of
date one day before the date of pre bid meeting on email id mayur
ahari@bharatpetroleum.in or anandkp@bharatpetroleum.in . You are requested to
inform the name of the person attending the meeting to Mr. Mayur Ahari,
Sr.Manager (E&C) / Mr. Anand K P, Dy. Mgr. (E&C) on email or phone nos.
022-3107 4232 / 022-3107 3223 well in advance so that the welcome slip for
entry of the person inside Admin Building is prepared in time.
Venue for Pre Bid Meeting: BPCL REFINERY, CPO CONFERENCE ROOM, 2nd FLOOR,
SOUTH BLOCK, ADMIN BLDG., MAHUL, MUMBAI 400 074
Time: 10:30 Hrs to 11:30 Hrs.
Note: M/s. EIL is consultant for this job and all future correspondence
incluidng tender evaluation/award and job execution/closure shall be done
under their advise.
DEVIATION:
You are requested to have all your queries clarified before bidding. However,
if you still have deviations from our RFQ and the attachments (specifications
/ scope of work / Drawings) etc. please indicate deviations in your offer in
the bidding form titled 'DEVIATIONS' attached in the etender giving reference
To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
to RFQ clause no. Deviations appearing in this attached bidding form of the
E-Tender titled 'DEVIATIONS' shall only be considered while evaluating the
offer. In case there are no deviations, please submit "NIL DEVIATIONS".
Deviations mentioned elsewhere shall not be considered for evaluation.
NEFT PAYMENT:
Payments to Bidders etc. shall be made by National Electronic Fund Transfer
(NEFT) only. Payment by cheque will be made only to those Bidders whose bank
is located in the city where NEFT payment facility is presently not available.
Bids received from Bidders who are not accepting the NEFT payment terms are
likely to be rejected.
In case of any technical clarification, please contact BPCL
Engineer-in-charge.
1. Mr. Mayur Ahari
Sr Manager (E&C), MR
Email: mayurahari@bharatpetroleum.in
1. Ms. Anand K P
DGM (E&C), MR
Email: anandkp@bharatpetroleum.in
In case of any commercial clarification, please contact.
1. Mr. Ranjeet Kumar
Procurement Manger (Projects) MR
Email: ranjeetkumar@bharatpetroleum.in
Scope of work : As attached
Other Conditions of Contract:-
1. The jobs under this tender is of 'Service' and non-divisible in nature. MSE
purchase preference will shall be applicable for this tender as per policy of
GoI.
2. For this service contracts, all bidders quoting as MSE will be required to
submit CA certificate along with MSE registration certificate / document (Not
applicable in case of NSIC) to avail the benefits under Public Procurement
Policy as per MSMED Act 2006 / Public Procurement Policy order 2012. The CA
certificate should be dated after the date of floating of tender and shall be
specific to the tender for which the bid is being submitted. Format of the CA
certificate is attached with tender for reference.
In case CA certificate is not submitted, bidder shall not be considered as MSE
and benefits under Public Procurement Policy as per MSMED Act 2006 / Public
To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
Procurement Policy order 2012 shall not be extended to them.
3. All MSEs who are having Udyog Aadhaar Memorandum (UAM) /EM I/ EM II shall
not be given benefits available under Public Procurement Policy for MSEs order
2012. They are required to file fresh registration in the new Udyam
Registration Portal (https://udyamregistration.gov.in) and submit the same in
tender for getting MSE purchase preference as per notification no S.O.2119 (E)
4. BPCL Mumbai Refinery is an ISO:50001:2018 certified Refinery and is focused
on energy efficiency. In line with the same, all Bidders providing services
for BPCL Mumbai Refinery should preferably deploy energy efficient equipments
such as energy efficient welding machines, LED temporary lighting and
temporary window AC of BEE 4-star rating and above for execution of the work.
5. The executed quantities of the SOR items may vary +/- any percentage. In
case of change in L-1 status based on concluded executed quantities, the L1
bidder shall offer necessary discount to BPCL to ensure its L1 status.
6. Bidders / Service provider hereby agrees to ensure that the contract labour
engaged by him / her for carrying out his / her contractual obligations will
be upto 60 years of age. In case of any deviation the Management reserves the
right to take suitable action against the Bidder / Service provider.
7. The Bidder shall be obliged to pay the wages through 'electronic payment
mode' in the personal bank account of his/her workmen deployed at the site of
BPCL, Mumbai Refinery sites. The wages shall be paid by electronic payment
mode within 7 days of the end of previous month.
8. It shall be the responsibility of the Bidder to provide documentary and/or
any other proof in terms of Payment of Wages and/or any other remittances to
workmen through bank account, to the Corporation i.e. BPCL. It shall be
obligatory on the Bidder to submit the aforesaid documents etc. as a condition
precedent for payment of bill / invoice raised as per the agreed terms.
9. The Bidder, being the employer in the relation to persons engaged/employed
by him for providing the services, shall alone be responsible and liable to
pay wages/salaries to such persons employed by him from time to time which in
any case will not be less than the minimum wage as fixed or prescribed for the
scheduled employment of "Construction or Maintenance of roads or Runways or in
Building Operations.
The Bidder shall be obliged to pay the wages by electronic payment mode to the
personal bank account of the contract labour within 7 days of the end of
previous month.
Hydra/Crane shall not be used for shifting material, equipment, exchanger
bundles, pipelines, etc inside refinery from one location to another location.
It can be used only for loading & unloading purpose. This is to be followed
strictly for reinforcing safety practices in the refinery. The existing Hydra
To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
Cranes can be substituted with suitable/equivalent hydraulically operated
cranes that are equipped with rear mounted boom and out rigger supports. Some
of the models having these features are mentioned below for your easy
reference :
a. Escort Construction Eqpt. Ltd. (ECEL) : TRX Series - K10, F15, TRX 1651,
b. Action Construction Eqpt. Ltd. (ACE) : FX 120, FX 150, 15XWE,15XWF, Rhino
BID REJECTION CRITERIA FOR TENDER:
The following provisions of the bidding document must be adhered to without
deviation, failing which the bid may be considered to be non-responsive and
may be rejected.
Bid Validity
" Non submission of Holiday Declaration
Time schedule
EMD/ Bid Security/SD/PBG
Security Deposit / Performance Bank Guarantee
Scope of Work / Scope of Supply
Price Reduction Schedule / Liquidated Damages for delay.
Payment Terms
Deviation to terms & conditions of RFQ / and its Addendum / Corrigendum if
any
Presumptions etc. in Price Bid and "terms & conditions of tender".
Defects Liability Period
Schedule of Rates / Prices
Bidder does not quote for any service item in price bid
Bidder quote "Zero" for any service item in price bid
Any type of price information in Technical Bid/ un-priced bid
" Non submission of undertaking for PPPMII
" Non submission of Land Border Declaration
" Non submission of undertaking for TPIA verified documents submission.
" Non submission of Integrity Pact.
Bidder shall always examine the drawings and work front released to them.
In no case the executed value of the job shall exceed the awarded value of the
contract. 'No Cost overrun and Time overrun' will be permitted on Purchase
orders awarded against this tender. Hence it is requested to take utmost care
while executing the job and ensure that, jobs shall be carried out within the
approved contract value and time limit. CPO-MR will not be able to honour any
payments that exceed the contract value. Additional payment will not be made
if the job executed is beyond the awarded contract value.
For engagement of Workers to carry out Jobs awarded under PO or Contract
the Bidder is solely responsible for prior understanding of the Process of
issuing Entry Passes and vehicle passes. Bidder has to apply online in the
To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
Bidder Management System (CMS) for all passes for his contract staff.
Following Mandatory Documents are required for the issue of access card
Aadhar Card.
All contract staff has to undergo Safety training which will be imparted by
our safety dept Free of cost
Police verification Certificate.
Medical fitness certificate carried out by the certified surgeon
Enrolment under ESIC/WC policy as applicable
PF enrolment is must. Form 11 is not accepted
Personal Bank account details are required for transfer of salary
Passport size photograph is to be uploaded in the CMS
The Bidder shall be solely responsible and liable for losses arising out of
delay and / or non-execution of the Job due to inability to produce mandatory
documents required for issuance of Entry Passes to his Workers.
Below mention is list of documents required for Vehicle pass entry (RFID*) as
follows :-
Copy of awarded BPCL PO (Front Page)
RC Book of Vehicle.
One Time Road Tax payment Documents.
Valid Insurance copy.
Valid PUC Certificate.
'T" Permit (Taxi Permit)
Permanent driving License of Driver.
Safety training Certificate.
A letter from User Department to issue IDL.
*RFID Of Contract vehicle will be issued by concern user Department.
14. Please note that all vehicles hired for usage by BPCL, or vehicles used by
contractor inside refinery premises, are mandatory to have "Taxi Permit".
Usage of personal vehicles for commercial purposes inside the refinery is a
violation under Motor Vehicle rule and if found guilty, action as deemed fit
shall be taken. This clause will overrule all other taxi permit-related
clauses mentioned anywhere in tender documents i.e., special conditions of
contracts, scope of work etc. Also, all vehicles shall have Yellow
registration boards as applicable for commercial vehicles specified by state
RTO, from time to time.
15. For getting entry inside BPCL Mumbai Refinery it is mandatory for each
contract person to have Access Card issued by Employees Relation department as
mentioned above. This access card is issued on a chargeable basis and charges
is nonrefundable. Recovery of this charges shall be done through debit entry
from respective Bidder account by BPCL. This charge needs to be considered
while submitting bid. BPCL will not make any payment for this in any form and
needs to be borne by bidder itself.
To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
There would be payment of Rs 400/- + GST (Per card, Non-Refundable) for all
categories of access card.
After the expiry of the PO new cards would have to be made, same card
cannot be used for a different PO.
Penalty of Rs 600/- +GST Per card would be levied for reissue of card in
case of damage/loss.
Above is subject to change and shall be complied by successful bidder.
Above mentioned rate are subjetc to change and bidder will have to complt the
same.
16. The service provider/contractor shall ensure timely wage payments to
contract labour as per the Contract Labour Regulation and Abolition Act, 1970.
The principal employer/buyer shall ensure timely wage payment by the service
provider/Contractor. In case off failure or short payment, the principal
employer/buyer shall be liable to pay wages directly and recover the amount
from service provider/Contractor.
17. It shall be mandatary for service providers/Contractors to first pay
salaries to the hired manpower before claiming reimbursement. This must be
supported by documentary evidence, including salary payment receipt, Employee
Provident Fund (EPF), and Employee State Insurance Corporation (ESIC)
contributions etc as per mandated by Law.
18. Addenda/Corrigenda to this tender document if any issued, shall form part
of this tender and signed and stamped copy of same must be submitted as token
of acceptance along with the tender technical offer, else offer may be liable
for rejection.
19. Contract Safety Management (CSM) System at BPCL Refinery:-
All participating bidders shall note that scrutiny of their past safety
performance and safety capabilities sufficient to meet BPCL HSSE policy and
proposed scope of work shall be carried out during the bidding stage as well
as work execution stage. The appended Safety Competency Form (Annexure - 1 &
2) shall be duly filled in and returned to us along with other applicable
tender documents. The completed safety competency forms shall be scrutinized
by a committee of BPCL. Hard copy of all supporting documents as called for in
the safety competency form shall be submitted to us by the successful bidder
only. For detailed process refer document attached in the tender.
20. Clauses for Land Border with India :-
I. Any Bidder from a county which shares a land border with India will be
eligible to bid in for this tender only if the bidder is registered with the
competent authority. Further ,any bidder (including bidder from India) having
specified transfer of technology (ToT) arrangement with an entity from a
country which shares a land border with India, shall also require to be
registered with the same competent authority (For Competent Authority
hereafter mentioned anywhere in para related to Land Border clause shall be as
To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
per Department of Expenditure O.M. No. 7/10/2021-PPD(1) dated 23rd February,
II.
"Bidder" (including the term 'tenderer', 'consultant', or 'services
provider 'in certain contexts) means any person or firm or company, including
any member of a consortium or joint venture (that is an association of several
persons, or firm or companies), every artificial juridical person not falling
in any of the descriptions of bidders stated hereinbefore, including any
agency branch or office controlled by such person, participating in a
procurement process.
III. "Bidder (or entity) from a country which shares a land border with India"
for the purpose of this tender means:-
An entity incorporated, established or registered in such a country; or
A subsidiary of an entity incorporated, established or registered in such
a country; or
An entity substantially controlled through entities incorporated,
established or registered in such a country; or
An entity whose beneficial owner is situated in such a country; or
An Indian (or other) agent of such an entity; or
A natural person who is a citizen of such a country; or
A consortium or joint venture where any member of the consortium or joint
venture falls under any of the above.
IV. The beneficial owner for the purpose of (iii) above will be as under
1. In case of a company or Limited Liability Partnership, the beneficial
owner is the natural person(s), who, whether acting alone or together, or
through one or more juridical person, has a controlling ownership interest or
who exercises control through other means.
Explanation-
a. "Controlling ownership interest" means ownership of or entitlement to more
than twenty-five percent of shares or capital or profits of the company;
b. "Control" shall include the right to appoint majority of the directors or
to control the management or policy decisions including by virtue of their
shareholding or management rights or shareholders agreements or voting
agreements;
2. In case of a partnership firm, the beneficial owner is the natural
person(s) who, whether acting alone or together, or through one or more
juridical person, has ownership of entitlement to more than fifteen percent of
capital or profits of the partnership;
3. In case of an unincorporated association or body of individuals , the
beneficial owner is the natural person(s), who, whether acting alone or
together, or through one or more juridical person, has ownership of or
entitlement to more than fifteen percent of the property or capital or profits
of such association or body of individuals;
4. Where no natural person is identified under (1) or (2) or (3) above, the
beneficial owner is the relevant natural person who holds the position of
senior managing official;
5. In case of a trust, the identification of beneficial owner(s) shall include
identification of the author of the trust, the trustee, the beneficiaries with
fifteen percent or more interest in the trust and any other natural person
exercising ultimate effective control over the trust through a chain of
To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
control or ownership.
V. An agent is a person employed to do any act for another, or to represent
another in dealings with third person.
VI. The registration shall be valid at the time of submission of bid and at
the time of acceptance of bid.
VII. If the bidder was validly registered at the time of acceptance /
placement of order, registration shall not be a relevant consideration during
contract execution.
Public Procurement (Preference to Make in India) (PPP-MII):-
Public Procurement (Preference to Make in India) (PPP-MII) (wherever PP-LC is
mentioned below in this documents or anywhere in tender to be read as PPP-MII)
shall be applicable for this tender as per MoP&G letter no.
FP-20013/24/2017-FP-PNG (E-17013) dated 21st August 2024 as attached herewith
tender documents. Any subsequent revision or new guidelines if applicable,
shall be taken into consideration while extending the purchase preference.
As per para 2.5, 4.4 (b) and 4.4 (c) of letter referred in para 1 above,
Non-Local supplier shall not be eligible to bid for this tender. Also purchase
preference shall be extended to eligible Class-I Local supplier only. Class
II/III local supplier shall not get any purchase preference. However tender
shall be non-divisible in nature.
For this tender mandatory minimum LC requirement is 20% and margin of purchase
preference shall be applicable as per GOI latest guidelines, and tender is non
divisible in nature.
Bidder declaring local content less than 20%, their offer shall be rejected
and shall not be considered for price bid opening.
For detailed PPPMII clause refer tender attachment.
Please note that for this tender PPPMII as well as MSE purchase, both are
applicable. However, based on the documents submitted by the bidder along with
technical proposal, either MSE or PPPMII or both, purchase preference may be
extended, subject to meeting the purchase preference criteria.
ONE BID PER BIDDER:-
A Bidder shall submit only one bid in a particular bidding process (unless
otherwise allowed in the bid Special Conditions of Contract). In case of a
holding company having more than one independent manufacturing units or more
than one unit having common business ownership / management, only one unit
should quote. Similar restrictions shall apply to closely related sister
companies. Bidder's sister/ Associated/ Allied concern(s) participating or
applying against the same tender, shall lead to disqualification of Bidders.
Sister / Associated / Allied concern means a company, society, partnership
firm or proprietorship firm having one or more common persons as Director /
Partner/ Member/ Owner. A Bidder who submits more than one bid will cause all
To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
the proposals submitted in the particular bid to be disqualified. In relation
to the above, a person will include firm(s) of Proprietorship / Partnership
Firm / Limited Liability Partnership / Private Limited / Limited company /
Society registered under Society's Act / Statutory Bodies / any other legal
entity, as the case may be and will be deemed to have submitted multiple bids
in a particular bid if a person bids in any of the two formats given below:
a) individual or proprietorship format and/or
b) a partnership or association of persons format and/or
c) a company format
Whereby,
a) A company shall for this purpose include any artificial person whether
constituted under the
Indian laws or of any other country.
b) A person shall be deemed to have bid in a partnership format or in
association of persons
format if he is a partner of the firm which has submitted the bid or is a
member of any
association of persons which has submitted a bid.
c) A person shall be deemed to have bid in a company format if the person
holds:
i) More than 10% of the voting share capital of the company which has
submitted a bid,
or
ii) Is a director and / or Key Managerial Personnel of the company which has
submitted
a bid,
or
iii) Holds more than 10% of voting share capital in and/or is a director and /
or Key
Managerial Personnel of a holding company of that company which has submitted
the
bid.
While making a bid pursuant to the Tender Documents, the bidder / tenderer
shall submit a declaration that the bidder / tenderer has not made any other
bid or multiple bids as understood or deemed in terms of this clause. All the
bids of a bidder who has submitted multiple bids, as per the clause, shall be
rejected and Bid Security / EMD for all such bids shall be forfeited. In
addition to the above, bidders found to be in contravention to the said clause
will be liable for Holiday Listing.
Wherever considered necessary reference should be drawn from the applicable
Act / Circular and Guidelines issued by the Statutory Bodies.
Dispute Resolution: Shall be applicable as per clause no 94 of General
Conditions of Contract (GCC) with venue of mediation proceedings at Mumbai
(India).
To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
ORDER OF PRECEDENCE FOR WORKS / SERVICES CONTRACTS:-
1. Contract Agreement (in GCC)
2. Detailed letter of Acceptance along with its enclosures
3. Letter of Award / Fax of Acceptance
4. Job Specifications (specific to particular job only)
5. Drawings
6. Special Conditions of Contract (SCC)
7. Technical Specifications
8. Instructions to Bidders
9. General Conditions of Contract (GCC)
10. Other Documents
Additionally, any variation or amendment / change order issued after signing
of formal contract shall take precedence over respective clauses of the formal
contract and its Annexures.
Note:-
Bidder is required to submit price break up of quoted price (On GeM portal)
under price bid envelope as per BOQ attached with tender documents in PDF form
duly signed and stamped on each page. Total price of break up submitted should
match with price (Inclusive of GST) quoted on GeM portal - itemwise. In case
of any mismatch, price quoted on GeM portal shall only be considered for
further evaluation and award as per GeM policy.
BASIS OF EVALUATION:-
The tender shall be evaluated on OVERALL L1 Price i.e. Grand Total of all the
items together. Overall L1 Price shall be calculated equal to Total basic
quoted price plus GST applicable on the total basic quoted.
INTEGRITY PACT :-
Proforma of Integrity Pact (IP) shall be uploaded by the Bidder/s along with
the unpriced bid documents after the same has been duly signed (or digitally
signed in case of e-tender) by the same signatory who is authorised to sign
the bid documents. All the pages of the Integrity Pact shall be duly signed.
Bidder's failure to upload the IP duly signed (Digitally) along with bid
documents may result in the bid not being considered for further evaluation.
a) For any queries / clarifications on tender technical specifications /
commercial points and other terms and conditions of the tender please contact
as under:
Mr. Ranjeet Kumar
Procurement Manager, MR
Email Id : ranjeetkumar@bharatpetroleum.in
To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
OR
Mr. Sunil Pandhare
Procurement Leader, MR
Email Id : sunilpandhare@bharatpetroleum.in
Office Address :
Admin. Bldg. South block, 2nd Floor,
BPCL Refinery, Mahul, Mumbai-400 074
b) Only in case of any complaints not resolved regarding the Tender / Tender
Conditions, please contact following Independent External Monitors (IEM) :
1) Shri Atanu Purkayastha
Email Id - dratanu2011@gmail.com
2) Shri Pradeep Kumar
Email Id - pradeepawasthi1981@gmail.com
3) Shri Javeed Ahmad
Email Id - Javeed60@yahoo.com
Attachments:-
1) General Conditions of Contract (GCC).
2) General Purchase Condition (GPC)
3) Bid qualification criteria (BQC) and EMD (Earnest money deposit)
4) Bidder Declaration form for Single bid
5) General Terms and Conditions
6) Financial Deterrent for violation of Safety norms by Bidders
7) Energy Policy
8) QEHSS Policy
9) Policy for Holiday Listing of Vendors in BPCL - As per GCC clause no 10
Format for MSE Certification by CA
SCOPE OF JOB FOR LSTK CONTRACT FOR F101 CDU3 HEATER REVAMP
Drawings
Undertaking for TPIA verified documents submission
Format for undertaking from TPIA - To be submitted by successful bidder
after price bid opening.
Certificate for Land Border Declaration
Integrity Pact
PPP-MII clause & undertaking format
List of Banks for BG acceptance
Stamp Duty charges for Physical Bank Guarantee and Cover letter with
Checklist
Contractor Safety Evaluation System
21) JOB SPECIFICATION, SCOPE OF SUPPLY & WORK FOR CDU3 F-101 HEATER REVAMP
To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
Any other documents attached with tender shall form integral part of this
tender.

text.b: Supply and Installation of New CAPH & GA
The item 00010 covers the following services:

page.text: LS
MECH GEN DSGN-ENG-SUPLYNISTLN
(Lumpsum)
DESIGN, DETAILED ENGINEERING, SUPPLY AND INSTALLATION OF EQUIPMENTS FOR THE
JOB AS DETAILED IN THE SCOPE OF WORK.
Service Item 10: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________

text.b: State GST % : ________ % Central GST % : ________ % Integrated GST % : ________ %
Union Ter. GST % : ________ % GST Comp. Cess % : ________ %
Revamp of radiant section including asso
The item 00020 covers the following services:

page.text: EA

The item 00050 covers the following services:

page.text: IND
PIPING FABnWELD NON IBR CS-TO SCH 80 (Inch Diameter)

PIPE LINES INCLUDING ELBOWS, TEES, REDUCERS, VALVES AND FITTINGS FOR ALL
HEIGHTS and ELEVATIONS. (UPTO SCH 80)

IND

ON PIPELINES/EQUIPMENTS - CARBON STEEL - NON IBR

PIPING PAD WELDG CS
(Inch Diameter)

REINFORCEMENT/CORROSSION PADS ON PIPELINES FOR ALL HEIGHTS INCLUSIVE OF

INM
PIPING FAB/WLD/ERECN STEAM TRACING (Inch Meter)
RATE PER INCH METER LENGTH FOR FABRICATION ,WELDING AND ERECTION OF STEM
TRACING LINE OF 1/2 IN.DIA TO 1 IN. DIA. PIPES WITH LUGS, CLAMPS, STEAM TRAPS,
VALVES ETC. AT ALL HEIGHTS FOR NEW/OLD/EXISTING PIPELINES.

IND
PIPING FABnWELD IBR CS-TO SCH 80
(Inch Diameter)
To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.

RATE PER INCH DIA OF WELD JOINTS FOR FABRICATION OF CARBON STEEL (IBR) PIPE
LINES INCLUDING ELBOWS, TEES, REDUCERS, VALVES AND FITTINGS FOR ALL HEIGHTS
and ELEVATIONS. (UPTO SCH 80)
Service Item 100: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
IND
PIPING FILLET WELDG IBR CS
(Inch Diameter)
WELDING OF WELDOLETS, SOCKOLETS, COUPLINGS, FLANGES, VALVES AND FILLET JOINTS
ON PIPELINES/EQUIPMENTS - CARBON STEEL - IBR
Service Item 110: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
INM
PIPING FAB/WLD/ERECN IBR CS
(Inch Meter)
"Fabrication of Carbon steel piping-IBR-Transportation of all piping items
from owners storage point to work site / work shop, fabrication including
cutting, edge preparation (inclusive of grinding the edges of pipe, fittings,
flanges etc. to match with the mating edges of uneven / different thickness
wherever required), fitup, bending, preheating wherever required, welding,
threading, and laying of pipes of all types and thickness over sleepers,
overhead on racks and at all elevations, connecting with equipment nozzles,
strainers, orifice assemblies, spray nozzles, rotameters, tapping for pressure
gauges, thermowells, sample connection etc. including welding of wear
pads/shoes as specified.
fixing of gaskets bolts, nuts wherever required and all other inline
instruments and fittings like elbows, reducers, tees, drains (but excluding
reinforcing pads) alignment, cleaning and flushing by water / compressed air,
hydrostatic, pneumatic, vacuum and any other type of testing as specified
draining, drying by compressed air, completing all such works in all respects
(including preparation of small bore isometrics and approvals from GBI/IBR) as
per the specification, drawings and instruction of Engr-in-charge. This is
exclusive of steam tracer lines.
Coordination/obtaining necessary approval from IBR/GBI authorities is also in
the scope."
To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
Service Item 120: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
INM
PIPING DISMNTLG WELDED PIPNG
(Inch Meter)
RATE PER INM FOR DISMANTLING OF PIPE LINES BY GAS CUTTING, COLD CUTTING
INCLUDING FITTINGS; ELBOWS ;REDUCERS; OPENING OF FLANGE JOINTS ETC ALONG WITH
STRUCTURAL PIPE SUPPORTS AS SADDLES; SHOES; HANGERS;ANCHORS; CLAMPS ETC. AND
TRANSPORTING TO SALVAGE YARD/WAREHOUSE. DISMANTLING FOR ALL HEIGHTS.
Service Item 130: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
INM
PIPING ERECTION CS/AS/SS
(Inch Meter)
RATE PER INCH METER LENGTH FOR ERECTION / HYDROTESTING / COMMISSIONING AND
FLUSHING OF PIPELINES INCLUDING ALL FITTINGS SOCKET WELD VALVES AND MAKING UP
OF FLANGE JOINTS/SUPPORTS/CLAMPS ETC PIPING ERECTION FOR ALL HEIGHTS and
ELEVATIONS.
Service Item 140: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
IND
MECH GEN SPADG/DESPAD FLG RTG UPTO 900#
(Inch Diameter)
SPADING/DESPADING - FOR FLANGE RATINGS UPTO 900# - RATE IN INCH DIA - SPADING
OR DESPADING ,WEDGE OPENING IF REQUIRED, REMAKING OF FLANGE JOINT, GRAPHITING
IF REQD, (FOR SINGLE OPERATION). FOR ALL HEIGHTS and ELEVATIONS AS DIRECTED BY
THE ENG IN CHARGE. RATING UPTO 900#.
Service Item 150: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
INM
MECH GEN TEST HYDRO TEST-INM
(Inch Meter)
JOB INVOLVES CARRYING OUT HYDROTEST OF PIPING/TUBES ETC AT THE SPECIFIED
PRESSURE INCLUDING ARRANGING HYDRO TESTING PUMPS AND ACCESSORIES, ATTENDING
LEAKY FLANGE JOINTS, VALVE GLAND PACKING ETC, OFFERING THE SAME FOR INSPECTION
AND CLEARING THE SAME, AS PER THE DIRECTIONS OF THE ENGINEER IN CHARGE,
INCLUSIVE OF DEPRESSURISNG.
Service Item 160: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
IND
MECH GEN RADIOGRAPHY-IND
(Inch Diameter)
Rate per inch dia for radiography of weld joints at all heights and elevations
as per the directions of the Engineer in charge, inclusive of all
tools/tackles/equipments and other consumables and mobilization.
Service Item 170: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
PMT PER CPR UNIT2 IR192
(Shift of 12 Hours)
Charges for Close Proximity Radiography unit having Ir-192 souce as per
tender.
Service Item 180: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
IND
MECH GEN STRESS RELIEVING-IND
(Inch Diameter)
STRESS RELIEVING OF WELD JOINTS - To carryout stress relieving of weld joints
as per instructions of BPCL officer in charge of the job. Supply of all tools,
tackles, ELCB for 440 volt supply and manpower required for the job is in
To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
contractor's scope.Payment on Inch dia of weld joint.
Service Item 190: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
M2
MECH GEN SHOT BLSTG EXTERNAL SURFACES
(Square meter)
RATE PER SQUARE METER FOR SHOTBLASTING OF EXTERNAL SURFACES OF
EQUIPMENTS/PIPILINES/VESSELS/COLUMNS/TANKS ETC AT ALL HEIGHTS and ELEVATIONS
AS PER BPCL STANDARDS AND AS DIRECTED BY THE ENGINEER IN CHARGE.
Service Item 200: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
M2
PAINTG PRIMER+SURF CLNG ZINC BASED-1COAT (Square meter)
PAINTING
PRIMER
INCLUDING SURFACE CLEANING/PREPARATION
RATE PER SQUARE METER FOR SCRAPPING/THOROUGH CLEANING OF SURFACES AND
APPLICATION OF ZINC BASED OR EQUIVALENT PRIMER (SINGLE COAT) ON
PIPELINES/EQIPMENTS AS PER BPCL SPECIFICATION. SCRAP/PAINT FOR ALL HEIGHTS and
ELEVATIONS AS PER THE DIRECTIONS OF THE ENGINEER IN CHARGE.
Service Item 210: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
EA
HEATER REMVLnREFIX OF BURNER ASSY
(each)
Rate per burner assembly for removal and refixing as directed by the Engineer
in charge.
To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
Service Item 220: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
IND
VALVE REMVL/ERCTN UPTO 600# RTG
(Inch Diameter)
RATE PER INCH DIA. OF VALVE FOR REMOVAL OR ERECTION OF VALVES UPTO 600# RATING
INCLUDING CHECK VALVES / GLOBE VALVES / PLUG VALVES/GATE VALVES AT ALL
ELEVATION AND HEIGHTS AS DIRECTED BY THE ENGINEER IN CHARGE.(RATE PER SINGLE
ACTIVITY)
Service Item 230: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
IND
VALVE REMVL/ERCTN CV UPTO 600 # RTG (Inch Diameter)
"REMOVAL/REFIXING OF CONTROL VALVES UPTO 600# RATING.
RATE PER INCH DIA FOR REMOVAL OF CONTROL VALVE OF ALL TYPES and ALL SIZES UPTO
600# AT VARIOUS ELEVATIONS. INITIAL TIGHTENING TO BE CARRIED OUT WHEREVER
TORQUING IS REQUIRED."
Service Item 240: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
MT
PIPING FAB/WLD/ERECN SUPPORT-CS
(Tonnes)
RATE PER MT FOR FABRICATION, ERECTION AND PAINTING OF PIPING SUPPORTS OF
CARBON STEEL AS PER THE INSTRUCTIONS OF THE ENGINEER IN CHARGE.
Service Item 250: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
MT
STR STEEL WK S/F/Erect MS-Strl
(Tonnes)
To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
STRUCTURAL STEEL WORKS
Supplying,fabricating and erecting in position bolted/ welded structural steel
work at all levels including all built up sections/ compound sections using
M.S. rolled section/plates/chequered plates in various steel structures like
mono rail, brackets, cat ladder, beams, columns, trusses, bracing's,platform
or any other structure, including transporting structural steel to site of
work, cutting, finishing edges by grinding, fixing in line and level with
temporary staging and removal of the same after erection, grouting with
ordinary grout/Non shrink grout as specified, preparing the surface for
applying one coat of shop zinc phosphate primer and one coat of zinc phosphate
primer with two coats of aluminium paint at site.
Service Item 260: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
MT
MECH-STRL DISMLG STRLS
(Tonnes)
MECHANICAL-STRUCTURAL
DISMANTLING EXISTING DAMAGED STRUCTURES LIKE PLATFORMS,COLUMNS, BEAMS AND
TRUSSES, ETC. MADE OUT OF STRUCTURAL STEEL INCLUDING CONNECTIONS,
TRANSPORTATION OF SCRAP TO DESIGNATED LOCATION ETC. COMPLETE AS DIRECTED.
Service Item 270: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
MT
STR STEEL WK S/F Grating/Frame
(Tonnes)
STRUCTURAL STEEL WORKS
Supplying, fabricating and erecting in position bolted and/ or welded hot
dipped galvanised grating (fabricated as per PCL standard drawing) made out of
contractors own MS flats, tor steel clamps in walkways, platforms, stair case
treads etc. including cutting to required size, shape, making hole of required
size, making the edges smooth, getting it galvanised after fabrication etc all
completed as specified and directed. All material to be supplied by
contractor.
Grating/Frame
To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
Service Item 280: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
MT
MECH-STRL DISMLG GRTG
(Tonnes)
MECHANICAL-STRUCTURAL
DISMANTLING EXISTING DAMAGED GRATINGS FROM ALL HEIGHTS, TRANSPORTATION OF
SCRAP TO DESIGNATED LOCATION ETC. COMPLETE AS DIRECTED.
Service Item 290: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
RM
STRL S/F/R HND RAIL
(RUNNING METER)
Hand Railing Fabrication, Erection and supply of Material
Scope of work :
Fabricating, erection and fixing welded/ bolted type hand railing made out of
specified types of class `C' MS black pipe as top rail conforming to IS:1239
by heating, grinding the bends including carrying out site welding, if
required, taking all safety precautions as prevailing in refinery and
including painting with two coats of synthetic enamel paint over two coats of
red oxide/ zinc phosphate primer . Supply of material required for hand
railing pipes, mid rails, toe gaurds and vertical posts will be in
contractors scope . This item also includes repair of hand railings .
Service Item 300: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
IND
VALVE REMVL/ERCTN RELIEF VALVE
(Inch Diameter)
"REMOVAL/ERECTION OF RELIEF VALVES.
REMOVAL/ERECTION OF ALL SIZE AND RATING OF RELIEF VALVES FROM THE VARIOUS UNIT
FROM ALL THE LOCATION AND HEIGHT, ADEQUATE CARE SHALL BE TAKEN SO THAT NO
DAMAGES TO TAGS OF THE RELIEVE VALVES, IF ANY, SAME SHALL BE INFORMED TO
To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
RELIEF VALVE TESTING AGENCY IMMEDIATELY, PROVIDE BLINDS AT THE EQUIPMENT
NOZZLE FROM WHERE VALVES TO BE DISMANTLED. PROPER RECORD SHALL BE MAINTAINED
FOR ALL RELIEF VALVES FOR EASY IDENTIFICATION FOR ERECTION, ERECTION OF THE
RELIEVE VALVE UNDER SUPERVISION OF BPCL REPRESENTATIVE INCLUDING GASKET AND
STUDS AND NUTS REPLACEMENT, RE-INSTALLATION OF VALVES SHALL BE DONE AFTER THE
TESTING OF VALVES BY BPCL.COMPLETE THE WORK IN ALL RESPECT AS PER DIRECTION OF
ENGINEER IN CHARGE."
Service Item 310: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
IND
VALVE REMVL/ERCTN MOV
(Inch Diameter)
RATE FOR REMOVAL/ERECTION OF MOVS INCLUDING MOV ACTUATORS AS DIRECTED BY THE
BPCL SITE INCHARGE. PROPER CARE SHOULD BE TAKEN TO PREVENT DAMAGE TO THE MOV,
ACTUATOR OR ANY VALVE PARTS WHILE CARRYING OUT THE JOB. PROPER TOOLS / TACKLES
TO BE USED TO ENSURE SAFE WORK AT SITE.
Service Item 320: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
IND
MECH GEN HOT BOLTING-IND
(Inch Diameter)

BASED ON INCH DIA OF FLANGE/MANWAY/JOINTS.
Service Item 330: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
IND
FABRN SPADES
(Inch Diameter)
RATE IN INCH DIA- CUTTING OF SPADES FROM BPCL ISSUED MATERIAL AS PER REQUIRED
SIZE and RATING AS PER THE DIRECTIONS OF THE ENGINEER IN CHARGE.
To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
Service Item 340: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
IND
PIPING MISC ADAPTER FABRCTN
(Inch Diameter)
FABRICATION OF ADAPTOR FOR PROVIDING TESTING, FLUSHING, STEAMING, WATER
WASHING ARRANGEMENTS OF THE EQUIPMENT INCLUDING MAKING HOLE BY GAS CUTTING
INTO THE BLIND FLANGE, WELDING NOZZLE / COUPLING / NIPPLE FROM SIZE 1/2" TO 2"
AND CARRYING OUT NECESSARY FINISHING OF THE PARTS FABRICATED. MEASUREMENT
SHALL BE TAKEN AS NOMINAL DIAMETER OF THE FITTING IN INCHES.
Service Item 350: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
SCAFLDG WITH MTRL ERECT n DISMTL
(Cubic meter)
Rate per M3 for erection of steel tubular scaffolding of suitable lengths at
all heights and locations as per BPCL standards with Contractor's Scaffolding
materials. The scope includes dismantling of the same after completion of the
job.
Service Item 360: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
EA
MECH GEN REMVnREFIX HOSES
(each)
RATE PER CONNECTION FOR CONNECTING OR DISCONECTING AIR/STEAM/WATER HOSE
(SINGLE ACTIVITY). [PROVIDING/REMOVAL OF AIR EDUCTOR IF REQUIRED IS ALSO
INCLUDED IN THE AIR HOSE CONNECTION/DISCONNECTION ACTIVITY]
Service Item 370: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
M2
INSULN WTH VDR SCF REMOVAL
(Square meter)
INSULATION
WTH VENDOR SCFFOLDING
The job involves removal of insulation of all thicknesses /sizes on piping/
equipments/ including scrap cleaning of surface free of foreign material,
disposal of scrap. Payment will be effected for finished surface area of
insulation before removal. scrap shall be disposed as directed by BPCL site
incharge. Scaffolding if required shall be provided by the contractor with his
material for execution of the job and the same to be removed upon completion
of the job as directed by the Engineer in charge.
Service Item 380: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
M2
INSULN WTH VDR SCF REMOVAL-AL SHT
(Square meter)
INSULATION
WTH VENDOR SCFFOLDING
The job involves removal of aluminium sheet on all sizes of piping/
equipments, disposal of scrap. Payment will be effected for finished surface
area of insulation before removal. scrap shall be disposed as directed by
BPCL site incharge.Scaffolding if required shall be provided by the contractor
with his material for execution of the job and the same to be removed upon
completion of the job as directed by the Engineer in charge.
Service Item 390: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
M2
PNTG - W/O SCAF SYS-1-REV1 W/O SCAF (Square meter)

WITH OUT SCAFOLDING
PAINT-SYS-1 (REV-1) W/O SCAF

For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.

Inorganic Zinc Ethyl Silicate Primer - 1 x 65 micron
High build two pack polyamide cured epoxy - 2 x 100 micron
Aliphatic acrylic modified high solids weather resistant recoatable two pack
polyurethane (finish coat) - 1 x 40 micron

M2

Service Item 420: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
INH
PAINTG WK Sign writing 2 coats
(INCH HEIGHT PER METER)
PAINTING/ OTHER WALL FINISHES
SSign writing with approved paint in two coats at all height including
scaffolding, surface preparation etc all complete.
Service Item 430: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
MECH GEN CLNG HIRE CASUAL - 08 HRS (Shift of 8 Hours)
CLEANING
Rate for hiring casual labour on 08 hours shift basis.
Service Item 440: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
M2
FABRN BUTT PATCH TO 12MM THK INCNL PLATE (Square meter)
BUTT PATCH WELDING WITH INCONEL ELECTRODES. THE RATE INCLUDES WELDING WITH
ALL / ANY GRADE OF INCONEL ELECTRODE AS PER SITE REQUIREMENT AND AS DIRECTED
BY THE AREA INCHARGE. RATE PER SQ.M OF PATCH INCLUDING GAS CUTTING TO SUIT
SITE REQUIREMENT, EDGE PREPARATION, ERECTION AND WELDING PATCH PLATES ON THE
PIPELINE / EQUIPMENT INCLUDING PATCH WELDING FOR ALL HEIGHTS UPTO 12 MM THK.
PLATES.
To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
Service Item 450: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
M3
REFRACTORY APPLICATION MANUAL
(Cubic meter)
Rate per M3 for removal of refractory in equipments using jack hammers/
chipping and application of refractory to the required thickness as per BPCL
specifications at all heights and elevations including supply of all required
tools/tackles, and necessary shuttering/support arrangements.
Service Item 460: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
M3
REFRACTORY DISMNTLG BRICKS/CASTABLE (Cubic meter)
Chipping and dismantling of Refractory lining of varying thickness (made up of
bricks and castable), as directed by Engg. in charge, making the surface good
for refractory application/guniting, cleaning of the area of all the debris,
debris to be disposed as directed by the Engineer in charge, scrap generated
to be dumped to the scrap yard.This includes mobilizing required labour,tools
for carrying out the same with idle time,etc.
Service Item 470: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
M3
MECH GEN REFRACTORY-CERAMIC
(Cubic meter)
Rate per M3 for removal of ceramic blanket refractory, lock washer, moist
felt and SS foil in equipments and application of new ceramic blanket
refractory of required thickness including fixing of SS foil, moist felt, lock
washer and mastic compound as per BPCL specifications at all heights and
elevations as detailed in the scope of work.
To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
Service Item 480: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
M2
REFRACTORY TOUCHUP
(Square meter)
FINISH PAINT
MAINT WORK-1 OR MORE COAT
Refractory Touch-up Repairs Manual - This item includes carrying out touch-up
repairs of refractory lining manually upto thickness of 20-40mm. The rate
shall be inclusive of all materials and mechanical works. Scaffolding will be
paid under relevant item.
Service Item 490: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
EA
MECH GEN WELDG ON EQUPT ANCHORS
(each)
Rate per anchor for removal of old/ damaged anchors by grinding and welding of
new anchors using Inconel/other compatible electrodes as per standards / BPCL
recommendations.
Service Item 500: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
RM
REFRACTORY GAP FILNG
(RUNNING METER)
EARTH WORK
Filling
Refractory Gap Filling - This item includes gap filling with ceramic wool, CF
modules gap filling, brick joint mortar filling, crack filling, tube sheet
joints gap filling, expansion joints filling and all other misc minor repairs.
The rate shall be inclusive of all materials and mechanical works. Scaffolding
shall be paid under relevant item.
To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
Service Item 510: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________
M3
REFRACTORY APPLICATION GUNNITING-REFRY (Cubic meter)
"Refractory application by guniting
Guniting of Refractory of varying thickness, including providing expansion
joints (Ceramic wool) as directed by Engg. in charge, fixing polyethylene
sheet on cold face castable before hot face castable application if required
as per the instruction of the Engineer in charge, applying bituminous paint
over anchor tips to take care of thermal expansion of the anchors, carrying
out moist curing, etc., taking necessary precaution to protect healthinesss of
the existing refractory lining, cleaning of the area of all the rebound
debris, disposing of all the debris as per the directions of the Engineer in
charge. This includes cost of labour,tools for carrying out the same with idle
time."
Service Item 520: Unit basic rate in words ________________________________

text.b: State GST % : ________ % Central GST % : ________ % Integrated GST % : ________ %
Union Ter. GST % : ________ % GST Comp. Cess % : ________ %
Grand Total (inclusive of all taxes, duties/charges etc)

page.text: To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
The Delivery/ Completion period is defined as follows:-
1. The start date of the job will be the PO date and the PO will be valid for
one year period.
2. The subject heater revamp is tentatively planned during the turnaround of
CDU3 unit in the month of October 2026 with an expected start date of 1st
October 2026.
3. The shutdown duration will be for 30 days with available engineering days
of 22 for the heater revamp job. The engineering days will govern as the time
of completion for this contract.
4. The pre-shutdown activity of this job is planned to start from 1st of
August 2026 and the contractor should mobilize the required manpower and
resources for the same.
5. All the material in the scope of supply of the contractor should reach the
worksite latest by 1st August 2026.
The final shutdown dates will be intimated one month before the shutdown.
PRICE REDUCTION SCHEDULE (PRS) FOR DELAY IN DELIVERY / COMPLETION :-
In case the contractor fails to complete the mechanical work within the
stipulated period during TA, he shall be liable to pay liquidated damages of
1% of the total contract value of contract per day and or part thereof of the
delay, that the work may remain incomplete as per the time schedule, subject
to a maximum of 5% of the total contract value.
To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
Payment terms:-
1. Supply and Installation of APH
a. 50% against delivery of material at site
b. 15% against completion of Pre TA activities.
c. 15% against completion of jobs during TA
d. 15% against successful completion of the heater revamp jobs and startup of
the unit.
e. 5% against submission of documents
2. Revamp Jobs in Radiant Section including material supply
a. 30% against completion of Pre TA activities including material supply.
b. 50% against completion of installation during TA
c. 15% against successful completion of the heater revamp jobs and startup of
the unit.
d. 5% against submission of documents
3. Revamp Jobs in Convection Section including material supply
a. 30% against completion of Pre TA activities including material supply.
b. 50% against completion of installation during TA
c. 15% against successful completion of the heater revamp jobs and startup of
the unit.
d. 5% against submission of documents
4. Supply and Service Cost for electrical and instruments Jobs
a. 50% against delivery of material at site
b. 15% against completion of Pre TA activities.
c. 15% against completion of jobs during TA
d. 15% against successful completion of the heater revamp jobs and startup of
the unit.
e. 5% against submission of documents
5. SOR Jobs for Heater miscellaneous works
For other SOR items in the contract payment against submission
of the RA Bills as per tender condition.
This payment will be made after making necessary deductions as stipulated
elsewhere in the contract document for materials, security deposit or any
moneys due to the Owner etc.
SECURITY DEPOSIT TOWARDS PERFORMANCE / RETENTION MONEY:-
To ensure performance of the contract and due discharge of the contractual
To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
obligations, the successful contractor will have to provide security deposit
of 5% of the basic value of contract unless otherwise specified in the
Special Conditions of Contract. Amount received/retained towards this clause
will be considered as security deposit.
This Security deposit may be furnished in the form of Bank Guarantee /
Insurance Surety Bond in the prescribed format. The contractor shall have the
option to adjust any Earnest Money Deposit- (EMD) if paid by NEFT/RTGS towards
security deposit if he so desires or otherwise if submitted by way of bank
guarantee / Insurance Surety Bond the validity of the same to be extended
suitably as advised by BPCL.
In the case of security deposit submitted in the form of Bank guarantee /
Insurance Surety Bond, the Bank Guarantee / Insurance Surety Bond shall be
valid and remain in force till the contractual completion period (expiry of
the defect liability period- refer clause-74, 12 months form the date of
completion of work) and with a claim period of six months thereafter. The Bank
Guarantee / Insurance Surety Bond shall be in the form prescribed.
The security deposit will be retained till the successful completion of the
work and thereafter till the expiry of the defect liability period (refer
clause-74), if applicable. This retention money / Bank guarantee / Insurance
Surety Bond held shall be released after the expiry of the defect liability
period provided that any defects appearing during that period are corrected by
the contractor and subject to Clause 18.2 of GCC.
Vendors/contractors shall be asked to submit the SD within 15 days from the
date of notification of award and in the event of delay in submission of SD,
the contract can be terminated. However, if termination of contract is not in
the interest of the work/ BPCL, an additional time up to 30 days can be
allowed for submission of SD depending on merits of each case, beyond which
the contract may be terminated with subsequent actions following termination
as per tender/procedure.
The Bank guarantee if submitted shall be from any Indian scheduled bank or an
international bank of repute having a branch in India or a corresponding
banking relationship with an Indian scheduled bank. The security
deposit/retention money shall be in Indian Rupee in the case of domestic
bidders and in US Dollars/EUROS in the case of foreign bidders. In case of
Insurance Surety Bond, the same shall be from the Insurance company as
approved by Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI).
Process for submitting Bank Guarantee / PBG under SFMS (Structured Financial
Messaging System) mode as follows:
For inward BG in INR:
Vendor shall insist their bank for issuance of SFMS Bank Guarantee for faster
payments. Vendor shall provide BPCL's Bank Account No. & IFSC Code (Details
given below) to their Bank as beneficiary at the time of application for Bank
To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
Guarantee in favor of BPCL. Issuing Bank shall issue the Bank Guarantee & send
SFMS message to BPCL's Bank confirming the authenticity of Bank Guarantee who
in turn shall send the confirmation to BPCL. Vendor should ensure the
following for issue of E-bank guarantee.
a. The issuing bank is on SFMS platform.
b. SFMS Message type used is 760 COV and SFMS Delivery report /Message copy is
sent along with original BG.
c. For BG amendment, message type 767COV is to be used.
d. SFMS contains following details:
i. Beneficiary's bank name: ICICI Bank
ii.
IFSC Code : ICIC0000393
iii. BPCL's Customer ID : BPCL583493800
e. BG Issuing bank should send the BG issuance advice through SFMS to BPCL's
designated banker : ICICI Bank, Backbay Branch, Mumbai (IFSC: ICIC0000393)
f. BG Issuance advice should mention applicable Unique Identifier Code (UIC)
in row/ field number 7037of SFMS Delivery Report.
a. BPCL Location: Kharghar, Navi Mumbai
b. Head Office: Ballard Estate
c. UIC: BPCL583493800
g. The original BG should be submitted along with print out of SFMS Delivery
report from the BG Issuing Bank Branch.
h. SFMS BG will help in faster verification of BGs and prompt release of
payment to vendors.
Other Guidelines for Inward BG's, following bank details to be shared for
SFMS.
Currency of Inward BG Bank Account Details
a) INR
ICICI Mandate attached as Annexure II
b) USD
SBI EEFC Mandate attached as Annexure III
Bank Guarantee (BG) should be executed on Nonjudicial stamp paper/E stamp
paper of appropriate value as per the Stamp Act. Along with the tender
enclosed is a list of prevailing Stamp Duty Charges on Bank Guarantee in all
the states for ready reference is attached in tender documents.
All vendors are requested that the physical BG should be submitted along with
Cover letter and checklist attached as Annexure V and Annexure VI.
Criteria for acceptance and replacement of Bank Guarantee shall be as follows:
(1) Criteria for acceptance of BG
Up to 1 Cr. - Bank Guarantees from any scheduled bank (including nationalized
To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
banks, other scheduled commercial banks, scheduled cooperative banks and
scheduled regional rural Banks) as appearing in the second schedule to the RBI
act 1934 shall be acceptable.
Above 1 Cr. - Bank Guarantees which are issued by banks having long term
issuer rating of 'A2' from Moody's or equivalent from Fitch and S&P in case of
the foreign bank and long term issuer rating of at least 'AA' from CRISIL or
equivalent from CARE, ICRA and India Ratings in case of Indian banks, shall be
acceptable. In case rating for a bank is available from more than one rating
agency, the lowest such rating shall be considered. Current list of banks
meeting the stipulated criteria is enclosed as Annexure I.
(2) Triggers for replacement of outstanding BGs:-
Up to 1 Cr. - Any decision to continue/replace existing BGs considering the
categorization, financial health of the Banks, etc. shall be discretionary
with BPCL.
Above 1 Cr. - In case the rating assigned by any of the credit rating
agencies falls below stipulated level at any time during the tenor of BG, the
counter party will arrange to replace the BG at the earliest, at its own cost,
through any other bank meeting the criteria stated in (1) above.
All BGs - In case a Bank is under moratorium by RBI, BGs issued by such Bank,
irrespective of the amount of BG, must be replaced by counterparty, at its own
cost, through any other bank meeting the criteria stated in (1) above.
Clause no. 18.2, 18.3 and 18.4 of GCC shall be applicable in case of
contractual violation and details mentioned in clause.
Clause no. 25 and 26 of GCC shall be exercised in case of forfeiture of
security deposit and action when whole of security deposit is forfeited
respectively.
TRADE RECEIVABLES DISCOUNTING SYSTEM (TReDS):-
Trade Receivables Discounting System (TReDS) is an institutional mechanism set
up in order to facilitate discounting of trade receivables of MSMEs from
corporate buyers through invoice discounting by multiple financiers. Bharat
Petroleum Corporation Limited (BPCL) is registered with TReDS platform of the
aggregators M/s. Receivables Exchange of India Ltd (RXIL}, M/s Invoice mart ,
M/s. M1xchange, M/s C2treds, M/s DTX . The eligible MSME bidders can avail the
discounting facility by registering either in one or multiple TReDS platform
of the aggregators. It enables the sellers (MSMEs) to discount their invoices
through the aggregators to the financiers at competitive rates thus unlocking
their working capital swiftly.
To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
001 Quotation received after due date will not be entertained
002 Your quotation must specify exact amount or percentage discount,
GST, Charges, Transport, Packing & forwarding,
etc. as applicable.
003 In case you are unable to quote, kindly send the quotation with a
regret for our records. Non-receipt of a regret will adversely
affect your vendor rating.
004 No quotation will be accepted without your rubber stamp and
signature.
005 Your quotation must be strictly in enclosed envelope
indicating Collective RFQ No., RFQ No. and due date.
006 For all future correspondences please mention the vendor code
Collective RFQ Number & RFQ Number.
To:M/s. BPCL ESTIMATE MUMBAI - 400038 India (300648)
For Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd.
Vendor Sign & Stamp
